module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency — 'I know how to test, I know how to learn, I know how to encode.' Lecture-level mood target: the awe-shaped graduation move — 'I'm the practitioner now.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
Strategy-fidelity — no front-running of downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture is M6 closer. M7/M8 moods (awe+ownership, 'where is this all going?') are not resolved here. 'You steer' lands practitioner fluency without pre-empting optional-module content.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block: 'lands inside M6 module after Spot-gaps exercise + before Arc-retrospective exercise.' Correctly placed as a pattern-naming closer after exercises.
This lecture is a closer, not an opener before a contrast-mood exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture contains no fenced prompts. Forcing function for a lecture is the recognition moment, appropriately delivered via body prose.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No authored conversation cues, no 'compare with your neighbor,' no 'tell the room' shapes found.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — per phase + close (8+/10 per teaching beat).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 overall: 8/10. Opening beat (mechanical recap of M5 worktree decision) carries facilitator-premium risk at ~7/10 for self-study: procedural recall frame before graduation frame is established. Close: 9/10. 'The engineer you are at the close of M6 picks' executes the graduation move cleanly. No beat falls below 7.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Opening paragraph bridges M5 mechanic to 'four practitioners would wire it differently' without an explicit connective. One sentence — e.g., 'That gitignore behavior isn't a quirk to route around; it's the question the four readings below are answering' — would take the opening from 7 to 8 for self-study students arriving without trainer context.

rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 mood = practitioner fluency. M7 mood = awe + ownership. M8 mood = 'nobody plainly knows.' Lecture closes on 'I'm the practitioner now' — practitioner fluency, not M7 awe or M8 wonder. No early resolution.

Teaching moment: 'there is no canonical wiring; the student picks.' Delivered at 'You steer' section, line 21: 'Their guessed answers above aren't the lesson. The lesson is that you can read the question for yourself now and pick the wiring that fits how YOU work.' Reliable across persona-skill variation — the '(All guesses)' parenthetical at the top of the practitioner section mitigates authority-transfer risk. A fast reader who skips the practitioner section still gets the explicit teaching at the close.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Risk: student who reads only the practitioner section (not the 'You steer' close) could miss the lesson. Mitigated by section heading 'You steer' which signals the close. Manageable.

rules_evaluated.evidence: This lecture's role within M6 is the graduation closer, not the Big Idea carrier (that's the Spot-gaps exercise). The lecture honours the M6 mood contract (practitioner fluency) rather than restating the Big Idea ('Spot gaps, build the loop'). Appropriate role definition.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 is final core module. For core-only cohorts, 'You steer' is the arc close. For M7/M8 cohorts, the strategy notes practitioner fluency is 'what makes M7's deliberation feel earned.' Lecture does not need to name M7 explicitly — the graduation close is correct here.

stealing_the_mood: Opening paragraph (within phase 1) carries ~7/10 in self-study delivery: mechanical recap of M5 gitignore/cp mechanic precedes the graduation frame without an explicit bridge. Facilitator-premium gap — trainer contextualises live; self-study student bridges alone. Scored 8/10 at phase level because the practitioner-readings and 'You steer' beats elevate the overall phase.
